differentials.raw: Asymmetry of circle of Willis Pathogenesis:   arterial wall deficient in tunica media + external elastic lamina (natural occurrence with advancing age)

context: Etiology: (a)  congenital (97%) = "berry aneurysm" in 2% of population (in 20% multiple); associated with aortic coarctation and adult polycystic kidney disease (b)  infectious (3%) = mycotic aneurysm (c)  arteriosclerotic: fusiform shape (d)  traumatic (e)  neoplastic (f)  fibromuscular disease Risk factors: Aneurysm by shape:
